maxContractTerm: **No statutory maximum found.** Kentucky's substantive student-transportation statute (KRS 158.110, "Transportation of pupils," current eff. July 15, 2024 [Amended 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 9, sec. 1]) authorizes local boards of education to provide pupil transportation by "a board-operated transportation system, transit authorities organized and operating pursuant to KRS Chapter 96A, local governmental mass transit systems, and individual contracted buses and vehicles," but is silent as to the maximum term of any contract with a private contractor. The companion section on county supplementation (KRS 158.115, eff. July 15, 2008) and the bus-standards section (KRS 156.153, current eff. April 1, 2025) are likewise silent on contract length. KRS 160.305 ("Contracts for use of school buses to transport persons eligible for transportation services," eff. June 20, 2005) does NOT govern district-to-contractor agreements — it authorizes the Cabinet for Health and Family Services to contract with a local board to USE the board's buses to transport seniors and persons with disabilities when buses are not in pupil-transportation service; it is therefore not a private-contractor procurement statute. The two procurement regimes available to school districts under KRS 45A.343 (the Kentucky Model Procurement Code at KRS 45A.345–45A.460, as adopted by local board election; or, by default, the school-district Bid Law at KRS 424.260) likewise contain NO contract-term cap. The only state-level multi-year-contract statute in KRS Chapter 45A — KRS 45A.080 — is by its terms a state-government (Finance & Administration Cabinet) provision and does not reach local public agencies (re-verified text references "the secretary of the Finance and Administration Cabinet," "price contracts and purchase contracts of state institutions of higher education," and cross-refers to KRS 45A.100 [state small purchases]). The closest thing to a periodicity rule is administrative: 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 10 and 11 require a district to issue "a written contract each school year" and a "performance bond each school year" to any contracted bus owner, which functions as an annual-instrument framework but does not affirmatively cap the contractual term. Best summary: **No statutory maximum; annual written-contract and annual performance-bond requirements under 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 10–11; multi-year arrangements are permissible by board action subject to the annual-appropriations/non-appropriation principle (see KDE School District RFP Template, April 2025, "TERM OF THE CONTRACT" clause, and KSBA Procurement Regulations and Procedures § 3.26).**
rebidRequired: **Conditional** — turns on the procurement-code election under KRS 45A.343 and on whether the aggregate value of the contract crosses the $40,000 threshold. (1) If the local board has adopted the Kentucky Model Procurement Code, KRS 45A.365(1) requires that "[a]ll contracts or purchases shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding, which may include the use of a reverse auction, except as otherwise provided by KRS 45A.370 to 45A.385 [competitive negotiation, post-bid negotiation, noncompetitive negotiation, small purchases] and for the purchase of wholesale electric power by municipal utilities as provided in KRS 96.901(1)." (2) If the local board has not adopted the KMPC, the default school-district bid law (KRS 424.260(1)) prohibits any board of education from making "a contract, lease, or other agreement for … contractual services other than professional … involving an expenditure of more than forty thousand dollars ($40,000) without first making newspaper advertisement for bids." Either way, the rebid duty fires when a contract is being awarded above $40,000; the statute does not separately impose a "rebid-at-term-end" rule because it does not address contract term at all. The implementing regulation 702 KAR 3:135 (current; Section 1 applies to school districts not adopting KRS 45A.345 to 45A.460; STATUTORY AUTHORITY: KRS 156.070, 156.160) establishes the bidding procedures applicable to a school district that has not adopted the KMPC (sealed written bids; public opening; recorded reasons for rejecting low bid; etc.). Substantive transportation rule under 702 KAR 5:030 § 10 requires a "written contract each school year" with the contracted bus owner — i.e., a new instrument annually — but the regulation does not itself require a fresh competitive solicitation at each annual renewal. In practice, whether a fresh competitive procurement is required at expiration depends on (a) whether the next-year award exceeds $40,000 (it almost always will for a bus-route contract), and (b) whether any of the statutory exceptions applies (cooperative purchasing under KRS 45A.420; noncompetitive negotiation grounds under KRS 45A.380 — e.g., "single source within a reasonable geographical area," "emergency," "perishable foods," "licensed professional," etc.; or small purchase under KRS 45A.385 for contracts ≤ $40,000).
renewalMechanics: **Mutual written agreement, with annual-appropriations contingency.** Kentucky law does not codify a renewal-without-rebid mechanism for pupil-transportation contracts. The Kentucky Department of Education's official "School District RFP Template" (April 2025), Section 7 ("General Terms and Conditions — TERM OF THE CONTRACT") supplies the contract structure KDE recommends to districts (text re-verified verbatim from the official DOCX): "The initial contract term is expected to be from XX Date or upon Board approval (whichever is later) through XX DATE. Upon mutual agreement of the parties, the Contract may be renewed year-to-year going forward. Prices quoted herein or as negotiated by the parties are to remain firm for initial contract term. Costs for renewal periods will be mutually negotiated by the parties." The KSBA model procurement procedures (Procurement Regulations and Procedures, KSBA Policy Service System) provide the parallel framework. Re-verified verbatim from the official KSBA PDF: § 3.26.1: "Unless otherwise provided in the Board resolution approving the expenditure, multiyear contracts for supplies and services may be entered into for periods extending beyond the end of the fiscal year in which the contract was made, if funds for the first fiscal year of the contract are available at the time of contracting. Payment and performance obligations for succeeding fiscal years shall be subject to the availability of funds." § 3.26.2: "Before the Board may enter into a multiyear contract, the Director of Purchasing shall make a determination and findings that (a) the Board's estimated requirements cover the period of the contract and are reasonably firm and continuing, and (b) the contract will serve the best interests of the Board by encouraging effective competition or otherwise promoting economies in the Board's operations." § 3.26.3 supplies a non-appropriation cancellation mechanic with reimbursement of contractor's nonrecurring costs. No statutory CPI/index escalator, fuel-cost escalator, automatic rollover, or evergreen-renewal provision exists. KSBA § 3.27.1(b) imposes a model-policy two-year cap on contracts let under small-purchase procedures (≤ $40,000), but this is a model board procedure, not a statutory rule, and does not constrain contracts let under competitive sealed bidding.
statutoryCitation: Primary substantive transportation authority: **KRS 158.110** ("Transportation of pupils"), as amended 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 9, sec. 1, effective July 15, 2024 — official text at https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=54759 (Kentucky Legislature, KRS database includes enactments through 2025 Regular Session). Secondary substantive provisions: **KRS 156.153** (school bus standards; alternative non-school-bus passenger vehicles for ≤ 9 occupants; effective April 1, 2025 — amended by 2025 Ky. Acts ch. 151, sec. 2 [eff. Apr. 1, 2025], 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 26, sec. 1 [eff. July 15, 2024], and 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 35 [HB 447], sec. 1 [eff. April 4, 2024]) — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=56078; **KRS 158.115** (county supplementation, effective July 15, 2008) — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=3442; **KRS 160.290** (general powers and duties of boards of education). NOTE that **KRS 160.305** is OFF-POINT (Cabinet for Health and Family Services contracts for senior/disabled transportation use of school buses; not private-contractor procurement) — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=3708. Procurement authority is dual under **KRS 45A.343** ("Any local public agency may adopt the provisions of KRS 45A.345 to 45A.460. No other statutes governing purchasing shall apply to a local public agency upon adoption of these provisions") — official text at https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=22399 (URL corrected from Stage 1 — id=22400 actually returns KRS 45A.345). The definitions section, **KRS 45A.345(11)**, expressly includes "school district" within "local public agency" and § (22) defines "services" broadly; § (10) includes "board of education of a county or independent school district" in "legislative body or governing board" — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=22400. Competitive procurement floor: **KRS 45A.365(1)** ("All contracts or purchases shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding…except as otherwise provided by KRS 45A.370 to 45A.385") — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=42084; exceptions at **KRS 45A.370** (competitive negotiation), **KRS 45A.380** (noncompetitive negotiation — single source, emergency, licensed professionals, perishable foods, etc.), and **KRS 45A.385** (small purchases ≤ $40,000, effective June 29, 2023 — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=54080). Cooperative-purchasing authority: **KRS 45A.420**. For districts that have not adopted the KMPC, the default school-district bid law is **KRS 424.260** (effective June 29, 2023) — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=53787 — which requires newspaper advertisement for bids on "contractual services other than professional" exceeding $40,000. The administrative-regulation overlay is **702 KAR 3:135** (bidding procedures for non-KMPC districts; STATUTORY AUTHORITY KRS 156.070, 156.160; most recent certification effective June 9, 2025 — corrected from Stage 1) — https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/kar/titles/702/003/135/ — and **702 KAR 5:030** ("Pupil transportation"; STATUTORY AUTHORITY KRS 156.070, 156.160, 189.540, 49 C.F.R. 382.101-382.605; RELATES TO KRS 156.160, 189.540, 281.605, 49 C.F.R. 382.101-382.605; technical amendment effective Nov. 18, 2022; subsequent certification effective 4-9-2026 per Legislative Research Commission). 702 KAR 5:030 § 10 ("A district shall provide the owner of any school bus or special type vehicle that is contracted to the board for the transportation of the district's pupils with a written contract each school year in which the responsibilities of the contractor are clearly established") and § 11 (annual performance bond requirement) supply the annual-instrument framework for any contracted bus operation. Implementation guidance: KDE "School District RFP Template" (April 2025); KSBA Model Policy 04.32 "Model Procurement Code Purchasing" (Adopted/Amended 8/26/2025); KSBA Procurement Regulations and Procedures § 3.26 (Multiyear Contracts).
districtVariance: **Yes.** Because KRS 158.110, the substantive transportation statute, neither caps contract term nor specifies renewal mechanics, and because KRS 45A.343 makes adoption of the Kentucky Model Procurement Code optional at the local board's election, contract-term and renewal practice is fixed by district policy and therefore varies. Demonstrated variance from official district materials (re-verified): (1) **LaRue County Schools Purchasing Manual** (07/18/2016) provides, for local-bid awards, that "[t]he length of the contract will be determined by the Superintendent/designee as deemed in the best interest of the district, not to exceed three years in most cases," and, for small-purchase procedures, "[t]he contract shall be for a period of time that will provide the most beneficial market price to the local school district (not to exceed three years), provided no time period shall be fixed for the sole purpose of avoiding the requirements of competitive sealed bidding." Note: LaRue's manual still recites a $20,000 small-purchase threshold from pre-2023 KRS 45A.385 — the statutory threshold was amended to $40,000 by 2023 Ky. Acts ch. 152, sec. 1, effective June 29, 2023; districts that have not updated their manuals may be applying outdated thresholds, which itself evidences variance. (2) **KSBA Procurement Regulations and Procedures § 3.27.1(b)** (model regulation widely incorporated by KSBA-member districts) provides for small-purchase contracts that "no contract time period shall be fixed for a period of time exceeding two years nor shall any period be fixed for the sole purpose of avoiding the requirements of competitive sealed bidding"; KSBA § 3.26 ("Multiyear Contracts") has no cap. (3) **Kenton County School District's "Purchasing Procedures & Guidelines"** addresses competitive-bid thresholds and procurement methods but is silent on contract term — confirming term is left to discretion. (4) **KDE School District RFP Template** (April 2025) defaults to a board-set initial term with "year-to-year" renewal "[u]pon mutual agreement of the parties." (5) Districts have also elected differently as between KMPC adoption and the default Bid Law, which produces additional process variance even at the same price point. The recorder found no official statewide guidance or AG opinion enforcing uniform application across districts; variance is the natural consequence of statutory silence plus elective code adoption.
specialEducation: **No different state contract/procurement rule for special-education transportation.** The Kentucky Department of Education's "Guidance For Special Transportation In Kentucky" (Office of Special Education and Early Learning, Updated July 2025) expressly grounds special-transportation authority in the same statutory provision applicable to regular routes. Re-verified verbatim from the official KDE PDF: "When mandated by federal or state law, Kentucky's public schools provide free transportation services to students with disabilities due to a disabling condition or special program need. Whenever a student cannot be safely transported on the regular school bus route due to a disability, or when a student is transported on a special route in order to attend an approved special education program or activity, the district will provide transportation or contract with the parent to transport. These practices are pursuant to KRS 158.110 and supplement and enhance district transportation services." The guidance addresses Admissions and Release Committee (ARC) determinations of whether a student needs special transportation as a related service, the "Unique Needs" test, training requirements for transportation personnel, and incorporates the federal IDEA/Section 504/FERPA framework — but it imposes NO different state-law term, rebid, or renewal rule on contracts a district enters with a private contractor to provide special-education transportation. The federal Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (and related federal authorities — Section 504, ADA Title II, McKinney-Vento, foster-care Every Student Succeeds Act provisions) impose a substantive DUTY to transport eligible students and certain non-discrimination and qualifications standards, but those federal rules do not establish a state-law contract term, rebid duty, or renewal mechanism distinct from the general procurement framework discussed above. KDE's recent alternative-transportation authority under KRS 156.153(3) (added by 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 35 [HB 447], effective April 4, 2024; further amended 2025 Ky. Acts ch. 151, effective April 1, 2025) permits a district to use district-owned, leased, OR contracted passenger vehicles for ≤ 9 occupants under an alternative-transportation plan approved by the Kentucky Department of Education and pursuant to administrative regulations to be promulgated by the Kentucky Board of Education on minimum standards, insurance, route safety, qualifications, training, and drug testing — but the statute again does not specify a contract term, rebid duty, or renewal mechanic for contracted alternative-transportation vehicles.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **Dual procurement-code election (KRS 45A.343).** Every Kentucky school district must either (a) elect to adopt the Kentucky Model Procurement Code at KRS 45A.345–45A.460 by board action, in which case no other purchasing statute applies, or (b) operate under the default school-district Bid Law at KRS 424.260 with implementing regulation 702 KAR 3:135. The choice materially affects what process exceptions are available, but neither path imposes a contract-term cap on pupil-transportation contracts.
- **Small-purchase threshold ($40,000) — same under both regimes.** KRS 45A.385 (KMPC) and KRS 424.260(1) (default bid law) each set $40,000 as the dollar threshold below which simpler small-purchase procedures may be used. Both statutes were last amended in the 2023 Regular Session, effective June 29, 2023 (Acts ch. 152, sec. 1 and ch. 152, sec. 2 respectively). Because a typical route or full-service pupil-transportation contract will exceed $40,000, the small-purchase exception will rarely apply to a full bus contract — but it can apply to ancillary services.
- **Noncompetitive-negotiation grounds (KRS 45A.380).** A district that has adopted the KMPC may contract noncompetitively only on a written determination of: emergency, single source within a reasonable geographic area, licensed professional/technician/artist services (specifically excluding construction-management services from architects and engineers), perishable foods, replacement parts where need cannot reasonably be anticipated, proprietary items for resale, student-operated enterprise, expenditures on authorized out-of-boundary trips, supplies sold at public auction, group insurance, supplies at reduced prices producing savings, or certain private-developer sewer/stormwater agreements. "Single source" or "emergency" are the only ones plausibly applicable to a transportation contract.
- **Cooperative / piggyback authority (KRS 45A.420; KRS 424.260(3); KRS 156.076).** A local public agency may enter cooperative-purchasing agreements with other local public agencies and rely on the contracting agency's compliance with KRS 45A.365/.370/.375/.380. School districts also may purchase under state, GSA, or other-district contracts on a "price reduction" basis. KSBA Model Policy 04.32 (Adopted/Amended 8/26/2025) authorizes piggybacking on contracts of the State Division of Purchases, federal (GSA), cooperative agencies, or another school district's bid. In practice, districts frequently use cooperative bus-purchasing arrangements (e.g., KISTA bus-finance program). Cooperative purchasing reduces or eliminates the need for a fresh competitive procurement at expiration if a current cooperative contract covers the need.
- **Insurance and bonding (702 KAR 5:030 §§ 11, 22, 23; KRS 160.310; KRS 45A.435).** A district must require annual performance bonds from contracted bus operators; must secure liability/indemnity insurance for contracted vehicles at department-set minimums; and, if a construction component triggers KRS 45A.435 (>$100,000 construction contracts), must require performance and payment bonds at 100% of contract value. These are bonding requirements, not term requirements, but they constrain how a multi-year contract is documented (e.g., bond renewal each year).
- **Disclosure / compliance requirements (KRS 45A.343(2)–(4) and KRS 45A.485).** Every contract entered after July 15, 1994 by a local public agency — under KMPC or any other authority — must require the contractor and all subcontractors to reveal any final determination of a violation within the preceding five-year period under KRS Chapters 136, 139, 141, 337, 338, 341, and 342, and to remain in continuous compliance with those chapters for the duration of the contract; failure is grounds for cancellation and two-year disqualification of the contractor (and disqualification of any subcontractor). Drug-testing, background-check, and licensure requirements apply to contractor-employed drivers under KRS 156.153 (as amended by HB 447), KRS 156.990 (penalty for unlicensed transportation), and KRS 160.380 (background checks; also amended by HB 447).
- **Alternative student transportation (KRS 156.153(3); 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 35 [HB 447]; 2025 Ky. Acts ch. 151).** Beginning April 4, 2024 (further amended effective April 1, 2025), a district may transport students in district-owned, leased, OR contracted passenger vehicles for nine (9) or fewer occupants under an alternative-transportation plan approved by the Kentucky Department of Education, subject to administrative regulations to be promulgated by the Kentucky Board of Education on minimum vehicle standards, minimum insurance, route safety, pick-up/drop-off protocols, and driver qualifications/training/drug-testing (49 C.F.R. pt. 40). The statute does not specify a contract-term cap for contracted alternative-transportation vehicles.
- **Charter schools (KRS 160.1591 et seq.).** Public charter schools are subject to the charter contract framework in KRS Chapter 160; the screened authoritative materials do not document any charter-school-specific term/rebid/renewal rule for pupil-transportation procurement that differs from the framework that applies to traditional public school districts.
- **State-approval requirements / KDE reimbursement.** Transportation cost reimbursement under KRS 157.360 and 702 KAR 5:020 (program cost calculation) is calculated using the state's transportation funding formula; the formula and SEEK-cost-of-transportation reimbursement are administered by KDE's Office of Finance and Operations and do not impose any direct contractual term/rebid rule.
- **Federal Uniform Guidance overlay (2 C.F.R. Part 200).** Contracts paid in whole or in part with federal funds (e.g., IDEA, Title I, McKinney-Vento, ESSER) are independently subject to the procurement standards at 2 C.F.R. §§ 200.318–200.327, including the conflict-of-interest, full-and-open-competition, and contract-administration requirements; the KSBA model policy 04.32 incorporates the Part 200 conflict-of-interest standard. Federal Uniform Guidance does not impose a per-state contract-term cap.
- **702 KAR 5:030 § 10 annual-instrument framework.** This is the closest thing to a "term" rule in Kentucky and is regulatory rather than statutory. A district must issue a written contract each school year clearly establishing contractor responsibilities, and an annual performance bond. This is consistent with — and contemplated to coexist with — multi-year contractual relationships under KSBA model § 3.26, where the multi-year contract is the master agreement and an annual written contract / bond instrument is issued under it each year.
exceptionsUnresolved: All five fields resolved on primary authority. Maximum Contract Term resolves as "No statutory maximum found" with the regulatory-overlay note about 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 10–11 (annual instrument). Rebid Required is "Conditional" based on threshold and code election. Renewal Mechanics is "Mutual written agreement, with annual-appropriations contingency; no statutory CPI / fuel / evergreen / agency-approval mechanic." Statutory Citation is anchored to KRS 158.110, KRS 156.153, KRS 45A.343–.460, KRS 424.260, 702 KAR 3:135, and 702 KAR 5:030. District-Level Variance is "Yes," demonstrated with LaRue County Schools manual and KSBA model § 3.27.1(b) two-year cap on small purchases. Special-Education differentiation is "No different state contract/procurement rule" per KDE OSEEL's "Guidance For Special Transportation In Kentucky" (Updated July 2025). No on-point published Kentucky Attorney General Opinion addressing contract-term length for pupil-transportation contracts was located after searching the AG opinions database (the negative-search path was confirmed at the adversarial stage by a fresh search for 'Kentucky attorney general opinion pupil transportation contract term renewal school bus,' which returned no responsive AG opinions but the KARs and statutes already analyzed). The validation pass also confirmed there is no post-July-15, 2024 amendment to KRS 158.110 (HB 446 / 2024 ch. 9 is the most recent action, already captured). Search path: (a) Legislative Research Commission KRS database (apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/) for Chapters 156, 158, 160, 45A, 424 and the specific section-level PDFs; (b) Kentucky Administrative Regulations 702 KAR 3:135 (re-verified to most recent certification eff. June 9, 2025), 702 KAR 5:030, 200 KAR Chapter 5 index; (c) KDE Procurement Solicitation Resources, School District RFP Template (April 2025), and KDE Pupil Transportation Management Manual (2007); (d) KSBA Model Policy 04.32 (Adopted 8/26/2025) and KSBA Procurement Regulations and Procedures; (e) AG opinions search 'school transportation contract term renewal'; (f) KDE OSEEL 'Guidance For Special Transportation In Kentucky' (July 2025) for any special-education-specific term/procurement rule.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: KRS 158.110 (Transportation of pupils), as amended 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 9, sec. 1, effective July 15, 2024

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: (1) 'Boards of education may provide transportation … by means of a board-operated transportation system, transit authorities organized and operating pursuant to KRS Chapter 96A, local governmental mass transit systems, and individual contracted buses and vehicles.' (4) 'Boards of education shall adopt a transportation services policy based on the statewide transportation services policy guidelines.' Silent on contract term, renewal, and rebid for contracted operators.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: KRS 156.153 (School bus standards; non-school-bus passenger vehicles), as amended 2025 Ky. Acts ch. 151, sec. 2 (eff. Apr. 1, 2025); 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 26, sec. 1 (eff. July 15, 2024); and 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 35 [HB 447], sec. 1 (eff. Apr. 4, 2024)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Effective April 1, 2025; current through 2025 Regular Session
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: (3)(a) 'Districts may also use vehicles owned, leased, or contracted by the district that were designed and built by the manufacturer for passenger transportation of nine (9) or fewer passengers, including the driver, for transporting students to and from school and approved school activities under an alternative transportation plan approved by the Kentucky Department of Education.' (3)(c) Such vehicles must be operated by an employee or contractor with a valid Class D license; (3)(d) Kentucky Board of Education to promulgate regulations on minimum standards, insurance, route safety, qualifications, training, and drug testing aligned to 49 C.F.R. pt. 40.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: KRS 158.115 (Supplementation of school bus transportation system by county out of general funds), eff. July 15, 2008
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Authorizes a county to furnish supplemental transportation from its general funds, by 'local board of education operated transportation systems, transit authorities organized and operating pursuant to KRS Chapter 96A, local governmental mass transit systems, and individual contracted buses and vehicles.' Silent on contract term.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: KRS 160.305 (Contracts for use of school buses to transport persons eligible for transportation services), eff. June 20, 2005
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Explanatory only — confirms KRS 160.305 is OFF-POINT for private-contractor procurement
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: 'The Cabinet for Health and Family Services may enter into a contract with the local board of education of any school district in the Commonwealth for the use of school buses to transport persons eligible for transportation services [age 62+, persons with disabilities, others designated by CHFS] at times when the buses are not needed to transport students to or from school or school events.' This is an OUTBOUND-use authority, not a private-contractor authority.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: KRS 160.290 (General powers and duties of board of education), as amended 2010 Ky. Acts ch. 63, sec. 11, eff. July 15, 2010
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Broad grant of authority to local boards to manage school funds and property, adopt rules including use of reverse auctions under KRS 45A.070, and enter Interlocal Cooperation Act agreements. Silent on transportation contract term.

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Re-verified verbatim: refers to 'the secretary of the Finance and Administration Cabinet,' 'price contracts and purchase contracts of state institutions of higher education,' and cross-refers to KRS 45A.100 (state small purchases) and KRS 45A.225–290 (state procurement). By its terms a state-government provision; not applicable to a local school district's pupil-transportation contract.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: 702 KAR 3:135 (Bidding procedures); most recent certification effective June 9, 2025 (corrected from Stage 1's 11-16-2018)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: STATUTORY AUTHORITY KRS 156.070, 156.160. Section 1 applies to school districts not adopting KRS 45A.345 to 45A.460 and requires bid notices, written sealed bids opened publicly, prohibition on post-opening bid changes, board-minute documentation of reasons for rejecting low bid, public records access, and written award notification.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: 702 KAR 5:030 (Pupil transportation); technical amendment eff. Nov. 18, 2022; subsequent certification eff. 4-9-2026
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term (annual-instrument framework); Rebid Required; Notes; Special Education
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: RELATES TO: KRS 156.160, 189.540, 281.605, 49 C.F.R. 382.101-382.605. Section 10: 'A district shall provide the owner of any school bus or special type vehicle that is contracted to the board for the transportation of the district's pupils with a written contract each school year in which the responsibilities of the contractor are clearly established.' Section 11: 'A district shall require the owner of any school bus or special type vehicle contracted to the board to provide a performance bond each school year in the amount that the district deems necessary.' Section 23: 'If there are pupils transported on school buses or other vehicles contracted to the board, the board shall enter into an agreement with the contractor to secure fleet rates for liability or indemnity insurance for these vehicles.'

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Kentucky Department of Education, 'School District RFP Template' (April 2025; published 5/5/2025)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Section 7, TERM OF THE CONTRACT (re-verified verbatim from the official DOCX): 'The initial contract term is expected to be from XX Date or upon Board approval (whichever is later) through XX DATE. Upon mutual agreement of the parties, the Contract may be renewed year-to-year going forward. Prices quoted herein or as negotiated by the parties are to remain firm for initial contract term. Costs for renewal periods will be mutually negotiated by the parties.' Legal Requirements clause: 'any contract resulting from this RFP must comply with all applicable provisions of KRS Chapter 45A (Kentucky Model Procurement Code) or KRS 424.260 (old Kentucky Bid Law)…'

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Kentucky Department of Education, Office of Special Education and Early Learning, 'Guidance For Special Transportation In Kentucky' (Updated July 2025)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Re-verified verbatim from the official KDE PDF Introduction: 'When mandated by federal or state law, Kentucky's public schools provide free transportation services to students with disabilities… These practices are pursuant to KRS 158.110 and supplement and enhance district transportation services.' Discusses ARC determinations, the 'Unique Needs' test, training requirements. Does not impose any different contract term, rebid, or renewal rule for special-education transportation contracts.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Kentucky Department of Education, 'Pupil Transportation Management Manual: Promoting Safety and Efficiency' (2007 edition)
sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: 2007 (KDE has not posted a more recent comprehensive manual)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Chapter sections 'Contracted Pupil Transportation System' and 'Private Party Contracts' acknowledge district authority to use private contractors; references 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 2, 10, 11, 22, 23 and 702 KAR 5:130; supplies a general bid-procedure walkthrough using an outdated threshold ($20,000, now $40,000 by 2023 amendment to KRS 45A.385 and KRS 424.260). Does not impose a term limit.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Kentucky School Boards Association, Model Policy 04.32 'Model Procurement Code Purchasing' (Adopted/Amended 8/26/2025; Order #5997)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Re-verified verbatim from official KSBA DOCX: 'Purchasing procedures shall conform to the Model Procurement Code, KRS 45A.345 – KRS 45A.460[]. All contracts or purchases shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding or competitive negotiation, both of which may include the use of a reverse auction, except as otherwise provided by law.' 'District small purchase procedures may be used for any contract in which the aggregate amount does not exceed $40,000.00.' Silent on contract term.

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Kentucky School Boards Association, 'Procurement Regulations and Procedures' (model board procedures)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Re-verified verbatim from official KSBA PDF: § 3.26.1: 'Unless otherwise provided in the Board resolution approving the expenditure, multiyear contracts for supplies and services may be entered into for periods extending beyond the end of the fiscal year in which the contract was made, if funds for the first fiscal year of the contract are available at the time of contracting. Payment and performance obligations for succeeding fiscal years shall be subject to the availability of funds.' § 3.26.2: requires determination and findings that '(a) the Board's estimated requirements cover the period of the contract and are reasonably firm and continuing, and (b) the contract will serve the best interests of the Board by encouraging effective competition or otherwise promoting economies in the Board's operations.' § 3.27.1(b) (small purchases): 'no contract time period shall be fixed for a period of time exceeding two years nor shall any period be fixed for the sole purpose of avoiding the requirements of competitive sealed bidding or any negotiation procedures…'

sourceLedgerRows.citation: LaRue County Public Schools, 'Purchasing Manual' (07/18/2016)
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Re-verified verbatim: For local bids: 'The length of the contract will be determined by the Superintendent/designee as deemed in the best interest of the district, not to exceed three years in most cases.' Small Purchase Procedure: 'The contract shall be for a period of time that will provide the most beneficial market price to the local school district (not to exceed three years), provided no time period shall be fixed for the sole purpose of avoiding the requirements of competitive sealed bidding.'

sourceLedgerRows.citation: Kenton County School District, 'Purchasing Procedures & Guidelines'
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Addresses competitive-bid thresholds and procurement methods but is silent on contract term — confirms term is left to discretion.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: 'No statutory maximum found'
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: There is a maximum term, or an annual-renewal rollover cap, in KRS Chapter 158 (pupil transportation), KRS Chapter 156 (school bus standards), KRS 45A.345–45A.460 (Model Procurement Code as applied to local public agencies), or KRS 424.260 (default bid law).
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened KRS 158.110 (id=54759), KRS 158.115 (id=3442), KRS 156.153 (id=56078), KRS 160.305 (id=3708), KRS 45A.343 (id=22399, corrected), KRS 45A.345 (id=22400), KRS 45A.365 (id=42084), KRS 45A.380, KRS 45A.385 (id=54080), KRS 45A.420, KRS 45A.080 (id=22342, confirmed STATE-level), and KRS 424.260 (id=53787) by direct PDF download from apps.legislature.ky.gov. Searched for 'not exceed,' 'term,' 'duration,' 'years,' 'extension,' 'option,' 'multi-year,' 'annual termination' across all retrieved texts.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. None of the codified statutes imposes a contract-term cap on school-district pupil-transportation contracts with private contractors. The only annual-instrument requirement is in 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 10–11 (regulatory, not statutory).

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required: 'Conditional' on $40,000 threshold and procurement-code election
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either (a) rebid is unconditionally required at the end of every term as a school-specific transportation rule, or (b) rebid is never required because pupil-transportation services qualify as 'professional services' or are exempted by a transportation-specific carve-out.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened KRS 45A.365(1) verbatim (competitive sealed bidding default), KRS 45A.380 (noncompetitive-negotiation grounds), KRS 45A.385 ($40,000 small-purchase threshold), KRS 424.260(1) ($40,000 newspaper-advertisement threshold and 'contractual services other than professional' phrasing), KRS 45A.420 (cooperative purchasing), 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 10–11. Searched for any transportation-specific carve-out or 'professional services' classification of pupil transportation.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. KRS 45A.365(1) and KRS 424.260(1) both treat pupil-transportation contracts as ordinary 'contractual services other than professional' subject to competitive bidding above $40,000, with no transportation-specific carve-out. Exceptions in KRS 45A.380, .385, .420 apply on their general terms. 'Conditional' verdict is correct.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: 'Mutual written agreement, with annual-appropriations contingency'
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Kentucky law codifies a CPI/fuel/escalator/evergreen mechanism, an automatic rollover, or requires state-agency approval for renewal of pupil-transportation contracts.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened KRS Chapter 45A sections (esp. KRS 45A.343–.460), KRS 424.260, KRS 158.110/.115, 702 KAR 5:030. Re-opened KDE School District RFP Template (April 2025) Section 7 directly via DOCX extraction. Re-opened KSBA Procurement Regs §§ 3.26.1, 3.26.2, 3.26.3 directly via PDF extraction. Searched for 'CPI,' 'escalator,' 'fuel adjustment,' 'evergreen,' 'rollover,' 'automatic renewal,' and 'state approval.'
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. KDE's RFP Template provides only mutual-agreement year-to-year renewal with mutually negotiated pricing. KSBA model § 3.26 supplies the multiyear contract framework conditioned on annual appropriation. No CPI/fuel/escalator/evergreen/automatic renewal/state-approval mechanism exists at the state-law level.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation anchor at KRS 158.110 + KRS 45A.343 / KRS 424.260
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A different KRS section (e.g., a transportation-specific procurement section in KRS Chapter 156 or 160) supersedes or competes with KRS 158.110 and KRS 45A.343 / 424.260, OR the Stage 1 URLs do not resolve to the cited sections.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened every URL in Stage 1 by direct PDF download. Searched chapter index of KRS 45A.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Refuted-and-corrected (URL only). Stage 1 cited KRS 45A.343 at id=22400; that URL actually returns KRS 45A.345 (Definitions). The correct URL for KRS 45A.343 is id=22399, confirmed by direct PDF retrieval (text begins '45A.343 Local public agency may adopt provisions of KRS 45A.345 to 45A.460…'). KRS 45A.345 is correctly at id=22400. All other primary URLs (KRS 158.110 id=54759; KRS 158.115 id=3442; KRS 156.153 id=56078; KRS 160.305 id=3708; KRS 45A.365 id=42084; KRS 45A.385 id=54080; KRS 45A.080 id=22342; KRS 424.260 id=53787) resolve to the correct statutes and the substantive findings are unchanged.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: URL corrected; substantive citation unchanged; High confidence retained.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: District Variance: 'Yes' — demonstrated via LaRue County manual and KSBA model 2-year small-purchase cap
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statewide guidance or an AG opinion enforces uniform application across all Kentucky districts, eliminating term/renewal variance.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-verified LaRue County Schools Purchasing Manual via direct PDF; re-verified KSBA Procurement Regs § 3.27.1(b) via direct PDF; confirmed Kenton County School District's purchasing page is silent on term; searched Kentucky AG opinions database and AG website for any uniform-application opinion on pupil-transportation contract term/renewal.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. Variance is empirically demonstrated and no uniform statewide guidance or AG opinion was located. Variance is the natural consequence of statutory silence on term plus elective KMPC adoption under KRS 45A.343.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education: 'No different state contract/procurement rule'
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Kentucky law sets distinct term, rebid, or renewal rules for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento transportation, foster-care transportation, or contracts with vendors serving students with disabilities.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened KDE OSEEL 'Guidance For Special Transportation In Kentucky' (Updated July 2025) directly via PDF; re-checked KRS 156.153(3) (alternative-transportation authority via HB 447); re-checked 702 KAR 5:030 for any special-education-specific term/procurement/renewal rule.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed. The KDE OSEEL guidance expressly states 'These practices are pursuant to KRS 158.110 and supplement and enhance district transportation services,' grounding special transportation in the same statute as regular routes. No different state-law term, rebid, or renewal rule was located for special-education transportation contracts.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Currentness — Stage 1 stated 702 KAR 3:135 'Crt eff 11-16-2018' and 702 KAR 5:030 most recent amendment 11-18-2022
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: These regulations have been amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after the dates Stage 1 recorded.
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-fetched the official Legislative Research Commission KAR pages for 702 KAR 3:135 and 702 KAR 5:030.
challengeMatrixRows.result: Partially corrected. 702 KAR 3:135 now shows a most recent certification effective 6-9-2025 (with 7-year expiration 6-9-2032) — newer than Stage 1's 11-16-2018. 702 KAR 5:030 shows a subsequent certification effective 4-9-2026 in addition to the 11-18-2022 technical amendment Stage 1 captured. Substance of §§ 10, 11, 23 is unchanged. Sources Ledger updated.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: No substantive impact on the five fields; documentation refreshed; High confidence retained.

perFieldVerdicts.note: No statutory maximum located after section-level review of KRS 158.110, KRS 158.115, KRS 156.153, KRS 160.305, KRS 160.290, KRS 45A.343–.460, and KRS 424.260. Annual-instrument framework under 702 KAR 5:030 §§ 10–11 retained.
Conditional verdict holds. KRS 45A.365(1) and KRS 424.260(1) both confirmed verbatim; $40,000 threshold and procurement-code election under KRS 45A.343 control.

Mutual-agreement / annual-appropriations finding confirmed. KDE RFP Template (April 2025) Section 7 and KSBA Procurement Regs § 3.26 re-verified verbatim. No statutory CPI/fuel/escalator/evergreen/automatic-renewal/state-approval mechanic exists.

URL refuted-and-corrected: KRS 45A.343 is at id=22399 (not id=22400, which is KRS 45A.345 [Definitions]). Substantive citation set is unchanged; all other URLs verified. Per the verdict ladder, the refuted-and-corrected URL has been adopted in the field text. The five-statute anchor (KRS 158.110; KRS 156.153; KRS 45A.343–.460; KRS 424.260; 702 KAR 3:135 / 702 KAR 5:030) holds.
Variance evidence (LaRue County 3-year manual; KSBA model 2-year small-purchase cap; Kenton silent) re-verified. No statewide-uniform-application authority located.
KDE OSEEL guidance verbatim grounds special transportation in KRS 158.110 (same statute as regular routes). No different state-law term/rebid/renewal rule for special-education contracts.

verificationNotes: SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT: For each load-bearing primary citation, the official source was re-opened on its official URL during the Stage-2 adversarial pass: (a) KRS 158.110 was downloaded as a PDF from https://apps.legislature.ky.gov/law/statutes/statute.aspx?id=54759 and parsed; text matches Stage 1's quote (board may use 'a board-operated transportation system, transit authorities organized and operating pursuant to KRS Chapter 96A, local governmental mass transit systems, and individual contracted buses and vehicles' and is silent on contract term). History line confirms 'Effective: July 15, 2024; Amended 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 9, sec. 1, effective July 15, 2024.' (b) KRS 45A.343 was downloaded from id=22399 (URL corrected from Stage 1's id=22400, which returns KRS 45A.345); text confirms verbatim '(1) Any local public agency may adopt the provisions of KRS 45A.345 to 45A.460. No other statutes governing purchasing shall apply to a local public agency upon adoption of these provisions.' (c) KRS 45A.345 (definitions) at id=22400 confirms '(10) The legislative body or governing board… board of education of a county or independent school district…' and '(11) Local public agency means a city, county, urban-county, consolidated local government, school district, special district…' verbatim. (d) KRS 45A.365 (id=42084) confirms verbatim '(1) All contracts or purchases shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding, which may include the use of a reverse auction, except as otherwise provided by KRS 45A.370 to 45A.385 and for the purchase of wholesale electric power by municipal utilities as provided in KRS 96.901(1).' (e) KRS 45A.385 (id=54080) confirms verbatim 'forty thousand dollars ($40,000)… Effective: June 29, 2023; Amended 2023 Ky. Acts ch. 152, sec. 1.' (f) KRS 45A.080 (id=22342) confirms it refers to 'the secretary of the Finance and Administration Cabinet' and cross-references KRS 45A.100; thus correctly characterized as a state-level provision that does not reach local public agencies. (g) KRS 424.260 (id=53787) confirms verbatim '(1)… expenditure of more than forty thousand dollars ($40,000) without first making newspaper advertisement for bids… Effective: June 29, 2023; Amended 2023 Ky. Acts ch. 63, sec. 5; and ch. 152, sec. 2.' (h) KRS 156.153 (id=56078) confirms (3)(a) alternative-transportation authority verbatim and history 'Effective: April 1, 2025; Amended 2025 Ky. Acts ch. 151, sec. 2…; 2024 Ky. Acts ch. 26, sec. 1, effective July 15, 2024; and ch. 35, sec. 1, effective April 4, 2024.' (Stage 1 missed 2024 ch. 26 amendment; added.) (i) KRS 158.115 (id=3442) and KRS 160.305 (id=3708) re-verified verbatim. (j) 702 KAR 3:135 re-fetched from the Legislative Research Commission page: STATUTORY AUTHORITY KRS 156.070, 156.160; most recent CERTIFICATION effective 6-9-2025 (newer than Stage 1's 11-16-2018). (k) 702 KAR 5:030 re-fetched: §§ 10, 11, 23 verbatim match; subsequent certification effective 4-9-2026 in addition to the 11-18-2022 technical amendment Stage 1 captured. (l) KDE School District RFP Template (April 2025) downloaded as DOCX; Section 7 'TERM OF THE CONTRACT' text re-extracted and confirmed verbatim. (m) KDE OSEEL 'Guidance For Special Transportation In Kentucky' (Updated July 2025) downloaded as PDF; Introduction expressly grounds special transportation in KRS 158.110. (n) KSBA Model Policy 04.32 (Adopted 8/26/2025) downloaded as DOCX; cites KRS 45A.345–.460 and $40,000 small-purchase threshold. (o) KSBA Procurement Regulations and Procedures downloaded as PDF; §§ 3.26.1, 3.26.2, 3.26.3, 3.27.1(b) text verbatim match. (p) LaRue County Schools Purchasing Manual re-fetched; 'not to exceed three years' language and outdated $20,000 threshold confirmed verbatim. CURRENTNESS CHECK: No post-July-15, 2024 amendment to KRS 158.110 located (HB 446 / 2024 ch. 9 is the most recent); no post-June-29, 2023 amendment to KRS 45A.385 or KRS 424.260 located; KRS 156.153 has been amended through 2025 ch. 151. AG OPINIONS NEGATIVE PATH: Fresh search for 'Kentucky attorney general opinion pupil transportation contract term renewal school bus' returned only KARs and KRS, no responsive AG opinions; confirmed absence of on-point AG opinion. NET RESULT OF ADVERSARIAL PASS: One URL refuted-and-corrected (KRS 45A.343 → id=22399); two regulatory-currency dates refreshed (702 KAR 3:135 to 6-9-2025 certification; 702 KAR 5:030 to 4-9-2026 certification); one missed amendment recorded (KRS 156.153 / 2024 ch. 26); all five substantive findings confirmed.

adversarialVerdictSummary: confirmed (5/5 substantive fields); one citation URL refuted-and-corrected (KRS 45A.343 → id=22399); two regulatory-currency dates refreshed (702 KAR 3:135 → 6-9-2025 certification; 702 KAR 5:030 → 4-9-2026 certification)
